RL.K.5-Q4: Look at how the lines are written. Do they sound like a story or like a song?

RI.K.5-Q5: Look inside the book on the very first page where the book's name is.

RI.K.5-Q11: She turned the book over — what part of the book is on the back?

RF.K.1c-Q3: Try saying each word out loud and clap once for each one.

RF.K.3b-Q1: A LONG 'a' sound says the letter's name. Say each choice and listen for it.
RF.K.3b-Q2: Say each word slowly. Which one has the same middle sound as 'pig'?
RF.K.3b-Q3: A LONG 'o' sound says the letter's name. Say each choice and listen for it.
RF.K.3b-Q4: Say each word slowly. Which one has the same middle sound as 'bed'?
RF.K.3b-Q5: Say each word slowly. Which one has the same middle sound as 'bus'?
RF.K.4-Q3: Read the sentences again. Which action is NOT in the story?

K.L.4-Q2: Think about words like 'unsafe' or 'unkind' — what does 'un-' add?
K.L.4-Q4: If something is 'colorful' it has lots of color. What does '-ful' usually mean?
K.L.4-Q5: If a treat is 'sugarless,' it has no sugar. What does '-less' mean?

RI.K.10-Q1: A FACT is something that is TRUE. Which choice is about true things?

RI.1.5-Q1: There's a special list near the front of a book that shows where each chapter starts.
RI.1.9-Q2: Both books are saying the sun matters in some way. What word means 'matters'?

L.1.4b-Q4: If '-er' means 'one who does,' then teach + er means a person who does what?

L.1.5d-Q1: One of these means 'extra big.' The others are smaller than big.
L.1.5d-Q4: Three of these don't really mean happy at all. One means SUPER happy.

RF.1.3c-Q2: Magic E makes the vowel say its name. Try adding e to hop and read it.
RF.1.3f-Q1: Say it: one cat, two ___? The 's' tells you about how many.

L.1.1-Q2: A VERB is an action — something you DO.

L.1.1-Q4: When ONE person does something now, the verb usually ends in -s.
L.1.1-Q5: An ADJECTIVE describes what something is like. Three of these are not describing words.

RL.K.9-Q1: Different means NOT the same. Bear and Cat each like a different food.

K.L.4-Q3: Two dogs is more than one dog. The '-s' is doing that work.

RF.K.1a-Q5: Reading starts at the very beginning. Where is the beginning of a page — top or bottom? Left or right?
RI.1.6-Q5: The picture is filling in DETAILS the words don't show. What does the picture tell you?
RI.1.10-Q4: Tricky words usually name something new in the book. They are there for a reason.

RL.K.6-Q4: Think about who creates the WORDS in a book versus who creates the pictures.
